wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the VSocial project overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as a full-stack social networking application for virtual creators (VTubers, streamers, digital artists). Document the core value proposition, target audience, and key differentiators from mainstream social networks. Include the project's current alpha status, development timeline, and roadmap. Provide architectural overview showing the relationship between frontend (SvelteKit), backend (Node.js), and database (SQLite/libSQL) layers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of the platform's unique features like marketplace, freelance gigs, and real-time messaging.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for VSocial's architecture. Describe the four-layered architecture pattern: presentation layer (SvelteKit frontend), application layer (server routes), domain layer (business logic), and infrastructure layer (database and external services). Document system boundaries, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Explain the separation of concerns, modularity principles, and how different architectural layers communicate. Include system context diagrams showing VSocial's relationship with external services like cloud storage and email providers. Address scalability patterns, load balancing considerations, and microservice boundaries where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Flow & Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's authentication flow and middleware implementation. Detail the complete authentication lifecycle from user registration through login to session validation. Explain the requireAuth and optionalAuth middleware functions, including token extraction, validation, and session verification processes. Document the authentication middleware pattern used in server routes, error handling strategies, and unauthorized access responses. Include practical examples of implementing authentication checks in API endpoints and protected routes. Address token expiration handling, session cleanup, and security considerations for bearer token validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Overview & Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for VSocial's database schema overview and architectural design. Explain the overall database structure, domain organization, and table relationships. Document the libSQL/SQLite compatibility approach and engine-specific optimizations. Detail the modular domain-based organization (Users/Auth, Posts/Content, Stories, Messaging, Marketplace, Wallet, Gigs, etc.). Explain indexing strategies, foreign key constraints, and referential integrity enforcement. Include database initialization settings, WAL mode configuration, and foreign key enforcement. Provide architectural diagrams showing domain boundaries and inter-domain relationships. Address performance considerations, scalability limitations, and migration strategy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stories Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's stories management system. Detail the complete workflow for creating, viewing, and deleting ephemeral stories. Document the API endpoints including POST /api/stories for story creation with multipart/form-data support, GET /api/stories/feed for fetching stories feed, and DELETE /api/stories/:id for story deletion. Explain story expiration policies (24-hour TTL), media type handling (image/video), background color customization, and view tracking. Include practical examples of story creation with file uploads, caption formatting, and background color selection. Address technical implementation details such as file storage management, media validation, and database schema for stories table.

wiki_catalogs.name: Posts & Timeline System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's posts and timeline system. Detail the complete post creation workflow including media uploads, scheduling, privacy controls, and metadata handling (polls, location). Document the feed algorithm implementation, cursor-based pagination, and real-time timeline updates. Explain post lifecycle management, deletion handling, and soft-delete mechanisms. Include API endpoints for post CRUD operations, like/share/save functionality, and poll voting systems. Provide practical examples of post creation with different content types, privacy settings, and scheduling scenarios. Address performance optimizations for large-scale content delivery and content moderation features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Direct Messaging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for VSocial's direct messaging system. Explain the DM conversation creation process, automatic conversation ID generation, and peer participant management. Document the message CRUD operations including text messages, media attachments, and voice messages. Detail the cursor-based pagination implementation for message history retrieval. Cover read receipt functionality, last message tracking, and conversation metadata management. Include API specifications for conversation listing, message retrieval, and real-time message sending. Address security considerations for DM access control and participant verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Marketplace System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's marketplace system. Detail product listing management including CRUD operations, media handling, and categorization. Document the search and discovery functionality with filtering, sorting, and pagination. Explain the e-commerce workflow from listing creation to purchase completion. Include API endpoints for marketplace operations, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Cover product moderation, seller verification, and buyer protection mechanisms. Address technical aspects like image optimization, storage management, and performance considerations for media-heavy content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wallet Balance Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for wallet balance management functionality. Document the atomic transfer mechanism that ensures transaction integrity, including the _atomicTransfer function implementation with database transactions. Explain wallet creation, initialization with zero balances, and currency handling. Detail the balance update logic, concurrent access prevention, and rollback mechanisms. Include practical examples of balance inquiries, automatic wallet creation for new users, and currency conversion handling. Address edge cases like insufficient funds detection, race condition prevention, and database constraint violations. Document the wallet table schema, indexing strategies for performance, and balance aggregation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's user management system. Detail the admin interface for viewing, searching, and managing user accounts including user profiles, account status controls, role assignments, and bulk user operations. Document user verification workflows, account suspension procedures, and user activity monitoring. Explain the user listing interface with filtering capabilities, search functionality, and pagination. Include practical examples of user moderation actions, account recovery processes, and user data management. Address user privacy controls, data export capabilities, and compliance requirements for user account administration.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for WebSocket integration in VSocial's real-time communication system. Document WebSocket connection establishment, message broadcasting patterns, and connection lifecycle management. Explain how WebSocket connections are maintained for real-time messaging, notifications, and live updates. Include practical examples of WebSocket event handling, connection pooling strategies, and fault tolerance mechanisms. Address WebSocket security considerations, authentication integration, and performance optimization techniques for high-concurrency scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System & Design Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's component system and design library. Detail the component architecture, reusable UI components, and design system implementation. Document component composition patterns, prop interfaces, event handling, and slot usage. Explain the component hierarchy, naming conventions, and folder structure. Include practical examples of building custom components, integrating third-party libraries, and maintaining component consistency. Address component testing strategies, accessibility features, and responsive design patterns. Document the global styles, theme system, and styling conventions used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Containerization & Docker
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Docker containerization documentation for VSocial. Detail the multi-stage Docker build process, base image selection, and layer optimization strategies. Document Dockerfile best practices, security hardening measures, and resource limits. Explain container networking, volume mounting strategies, and environment variable injection. Include practical examples of local development containers versus production containers. Address container registry considerations, image scanning, and vulnerability management. Document troubleshooting common Docker issues, performance optimization, and debugging techniques for containerized applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for VSocial authentication endpoints. Document login, logout, registration, password reset, and session management endpoints. Include detailed request/response schemas for JWT token handling, refresh token mechanisms, and multi-factor authentication flows. Provide examples of authentication headers, token validation, and error handling patterns. Document security considerations including CSRF protection, rate limiting, and session expiration policies. Include client-side authentication flow implementations and best practices for secure token storage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Post Creation & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the post creation and management subsystem. Document the complete post creation workflow including text content processing, media upload handling, privacy settings, scheduling functionality, and metadata parsing (polls, location). Explain the multipart/form-data and JSON request handling, validation rules, and error responses. Detail the post lifecycle from creation to deletion, including soft-delete mechanisms and restoration. Cover media processing workflows, file upload security measures, and storage management. Include practical examples of creating posts with different content types, scheduling future posts, adding polls and location tags, and handling various error scenarios. Address performance considerations for media uploads and content validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Users Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Users entity model. Detail all user profile fields including username, email, password_hash, display_name, avatar_url, cover_url, bio, location, website, education, workplace, phone, birth_date, gender, relationship_status, category, and role. Document user authentication data, verification flags (is_verified, email_verified), activity tracking (is_active, is_banned, last_seen_at), statistics counters (follower_count, following_count, post_count, wallet_credits, wallet_balance), privacy settings (privacy_level, profile_visibility), and gamification elements (xp_points, level). Explain user roles and permissions system, account status management, and profile completion tracking. Include validation rules, unique constraints, default values, and indexing strategy. Provide examples of typical user data structures and common queries for user management operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for VSocial. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js >= 18.x, npm), step-by-step installation process covering repository cloning, dependency installation, environment configuration, database migrations, and optional seeding. Document both development server startup and Docker deployment options. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common installation issues. Include verification steps to ensure successful setup. Cover essential commands and their purposes. Make this accessible to developers with varying experience levels while providing sufficient technical depth for proper environment configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for VSocial. Document the frontend stack including Svelte 5, SvelteKit, Tailwind CSS, and build tools like Vite. Cover the backend stack with Node.js, Express-like routing through SvelteKit, and database technologies including SQLite and libSQL. Detail authentication libraries (bcryptjs, jsonwebtoken), real-time communication (WebSocket), and security middleware (helmet). Include version compatibility matrices, dependency relationships, and upgrade paths. Document development tools, testing frameworks, and deployment technologies. Explain rationale behind technology choices and alternatives considered.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for JWT token management in VSocial. Explain token encoding and decoding processes, payload structure, and cryptographic signing. Document token lifecycle management including creation, validation, renewal, and expiration handling. Detail the token hash storage mechanism for session persistence and security verification. Include examples of token manipulation, custom claims, and security headers. Address token rotation strategies, refresh token implementation, and secure storage practices. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common JWT-related issues and validation failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Entity Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for VSocial's core entity models including Users, Posts, Stories, Messages, and Conversations. Document each entity's fields, data types, constraints, and business rules. Explain primary/foreign key relationships, indexes, and performance implications. Detail user profiles, authentication data, and role management. Cover post lifecycle, media attachments, and engagement metrics. Document story expiration, reel processing, and media handling. Explain conversation threading, participant management, and message delivery. Include entity validation rules, default values, and audit trails. Provide examples of typical data structures and common query patterns for each entity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reels System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's reels system for video content sharing. Document the complete reels workflow including video upload handling, processing workflows, and playback features. Detail API endpoints for reels creation, management, and retrieval. Explain video processing pipeline, format requirements, duration limits, and quality optimization. Include examples of video upload via multipart/form-data, thumbnail generation, and metadata handling. Address technical challenges specific to video content including transcoding requirements, CDN optimization, storage management, and streaming protocols. Document reels-specific features like captions, music integration, and interactive elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Comments & Reactions System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for VSocial's comments and reactions system. Explain the hierarchical comment structure with threaded discussions, parent-child relationships, and nested reply functionality. Document reaction types beyond likes (love, wow, haha, sad, angry), emoji-based reactions, and user-specific reaction tracking. Detail comment moderation features, deletion permissions, and content filtering. Include API endpoints for comment CRUD operations, reaction management, and real-time comment updates. Provide examples of comment threading, reaction analytics, and community moderation workflows. Address performance considerations for deep comment trees and real-time collaboration features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Group Chats
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's group chat functionality. Detail group conversation creation, participant invitation and management, and role-based permissions. Document group metadata handling including names, avatars, and descriptions. Explain participant management operations such as adding/removing members, promoting/demoting moderators, and handling group leave scenarios. Cover group-specific message handling, broadcast notifications, and group-wide read receipts. Include API specifications for group CRUD operations, member management, and group message distribution. Address scalability considerations for large group conversations and moderation tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Freelance Gig Platform
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for VSocial's freelance gig platform. Explain gig posting system including service creation, pricing, and skill matching. Document the application and hiring workflow from gig discovery to contract execution. Detail the offer system, bid management, and payment processing integration. Cover freelancer and client rating systems, dispute resolution mechanisms, and reputation management. Include API endpoints for gig operations, application management, and communication between parties. Address technical challenges around escrow systems, milestone payments, and milestone tracking for project completion.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's transaction processing system. Detail the transaction types including transfers, tips, deposits, and withdrawals with their respective business logic. Document the transaction recording mechanism, including dual-entry bookkeeping for sender and receiver accounts. Explain transaction categorization, descriptions, and reference tracking. Include examples of transaction workflows, error handling for failed transactions, and audit trail maintenance. Address transaction limits, validation rules, and security measures. Document the transaction table schema, indexing for query performance, and historical data management. Explain transaction rollback procedures and reconciliation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Moderation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for VSocial's content moderation system. Explain the content management interface for posts and reels including content filtering, visibility controls, and removal workflows. Document the content moderation dashboard with sorting options, bulk actions, and content categorization. Detail the content deletion and restoration processes, trash management, and content history tracking. Include practical examples of content review workflows, automated content detection, and manual moderation processes. Address content takedown procedures, appeals handling, and content preservation for legal compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: RTC Signaling System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's RTC (Real-time Communication) signaling system. Detail the signaling server implementation for voice/video call coordination, peer-to-peer connection establishment, and ICE candidate exchange. Document the signal storage mechanism using SQLite, signal cleanup strategies, and conversation-based signaling isolation. Explain the POST endpoint for signal transmission, database schema for rtc_signals table, and automatic cleanup processes. Include practical examples of signal routing, error handling, and scalability considerations for concurrent RTC sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management & Stores
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for VSocial's state management system using Svelte 5 runes and stores. Explain reactive state patterns, store architecture, and data flow management. Document authentication stores, user state management, and application-wide state handling. Detail store composition, state persistence, and synchronization with backend APIs. Include practical examples of store implementation, subscription patterns, and state updates. Address performance optimization techniques, memory management, and debugging state-related issues. Document integration patterns with components and server-side rendering considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Orchestration & Docker Compose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Docker Compose orchestration documentation for VSocial deployment environments. Document service definitions, network configuration, and inter-service communication patterns. Explain environment-specific compose files, development vs production configurations, and service scaling strategies. Detail database service setup, reverse proxy configuration, and volume management. Include practical examples of service dependencies, health checks, and restart policies. Address secrets management, configuration templating, and environment variable handling across services. Document troubleshooting compose issues, service logs aggregation, and monitoring integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Core API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for VSocial's core social networking endpoints. Document post creation, retrieval, updates, and deletion endpoints with comprehensive schemas for post content, media attachments, and engagement metrics. Include timeline/feed pagination, filtering, and sorting options. Document user profile endpoints covering profile updates, follower/following management, and privacy settings. Provide examples of complex queries, cursor-based pagination, and real-time feed updates. Include rate limiting strategies and content moderation API patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feed Algorithm & Pagination
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the feed algorithm and pagination system. Detail the timeline generation logic, including user following relationships, content ranking criteria, and algorithmic filtering. Explain cursor-based pagination implementation, parameter handling, and performance optimization techniques. Document feed refresh mechanisms, real-time updates, and infinite scrolling behavior. Cover privacy considerations, content visibility rules, and personalized feed curation. Include examples of feed API usage, pagination parameters, and response formats. Address scalability challenges, caching strategies, and feed performance optimization for large user networks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Posts & Content Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Posts and related content entities including posts, post_media, post_likes, post_reactions, comments, comment_reactions, and saved_posts. Document the main posts table with fields for user_id, body, privacy settings, engagement metrics (like_count, comment_count, share_count), scheduling (scheduled_at), status management (is_pinned, is_promoted, promotion_score), and timestamps. Explain the post_media junction table for multi-media posts with media_url, media_type, dimensions, and positioning. Detail the post_likes and post_reactions tables for user engagement tracking. Document the hierarchical comment system with parent-child relationships, deletion handling, and comment reactions. Explain the saved_posts functionality for user content preservation. Include indexing strategies, foreign key relationships, cascading operations, and query patterns for timeline construction and engagement analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for VSocial's system design. Describe the layered architecture with presentation (SvelteKit), application (SvelteKit server routes), domain (business logic), and infrastructure (database) layers. Document the technology stack including Svelte 5, SvelteKit, SQLite/libSQL, JWT authentication, and real-time features. Explain architectural patterns like repository pattern, factory pattern, and observer pattern. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flows. Address scalability considerations, security measures, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams and component breakdowns. Document technology decisions, trade-offs, and constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architectural Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for architectural patterns used in VSocial. Document the repository pattern implementation for database abstraction and data access. Explain the factory pattern used for dynamic database driver selection. Detail the observer pattern implementation through Svelte 5 runes for reactive state management. Cover the middleware pattern for centralized authentication and security. Document the singleton pattern usage for database connections and configuration management. Include code examples demonstrating each pattern's implementation, benefits, and trade-offs. Address how these patterns contribute to maintainability, testability, and scalability.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's user role-based access control system. Explain the role hierarchy including standard users, moderators, admins, and super admins. Document permission checking mechanisms, role validation processes, and administrative access controls. Detail the requireAdmin function implementation and role-based route protection. Include examples of role-specific API endpoints, content moderation workflows, and administrative privileges. Address role assignment procedures, permission inheritance, and security boundaries between different user tiers. Provide guidance on extending the role system and implementing custom permission checks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Relationship & Association Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's relationship and association models including follows, likes, comments, saved posts, and user settings. Explain many-to-many relationships, junction tables, and composite primary keys. Document follow/unfollow mechanics, social graph management, and privacy-aware relationships. Detail reaction systems for posts and comments, engagement tracking, and sentiment analysis. Cover bookmarking functionality, saved content management, and user preferences. Explain relationship constraints, cascading operations, and data consistency measures. Include examples of complex queries involving multiple relationships and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Ephemeral Content Expiration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's ephemeral content expiration system. Detail the automatic cleanup mechanism for stories and reels content including timestamp-based expiration (24-hour TTL for stories). Document database triggers, cron jobs, and cleanup processes for removing expired content. Explain the relationship between content expiration and user privacy, storage optimization, and performance considerations. Include technical implementation details of datetime comparisons, background job scheduling, and cleanup automation. Address edge cases such as content modification after expiration, manual cleanup overrides, and monitoring expiration processes. Provide examples of expiration query patterns and database maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Following & Followers System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's social graph system including following and followers management. Document the relationship model, follower/following state tracking, and bidirectional social connections. Explain privacy implications of following relationships, mutual follow indicators, and social network effects. Detail API endpoints for follow/unfollow operations, follower/following lists retrieval, and social graph queries. Include examples of social discovery features, suggested follows, and network expansion algorithms. Address performance optimizations for large follower counts, real-time social graph updates, and privacy-preserving relationship queries. Document blocking mechanisms and social spam prevention features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for VSocial's real-time communication infrastructure. Explain WebSocket-based message delivery, signal handling for RTC connections, and presence detection mechanisms. Document the real-time signaling protocol for establishing peer-to-peer connections and managing connection states. Cover message broadcasting, real-time typing indicators, and online/offline status updates. Detail the WebSocket event system for message synchronization, read receipts propagation, and group chat coordination. Include implementation patterns for handling connection failures, reconnection logic, and message queuing during network interruptions. Address performance optimization for high-concurrency real-time messaging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for payment integration and external processor connectivity. Document the deposit and withdrawal mechanisms, including integration points for external payment systems. Explain payment gateway configuration, API endpoints for payment initiation, and callback handling for payment completion. Detail security measures for payment data, PCI compliance considerations, and encryption protocols. Include examples of payment flow implementations, error handling for payment failures, and retry mechanisms. Address refund processing, chargeback handling, and dispute resolution workflows. Document webhook integration for payment status updates and real-time payment notifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report & Appeal System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's report handling and appeal management system. Explain the report submission workflow from users, report categorization and triage processes, and automated flagging mechanisms. Document the report review interface for moderators including evidence evaluation, decision-making workflows, and action execution. Detail the appeal process for reported content including user appeals, escalation procedures, and final resolution tracking. Include practical examples of common violation types, moderation thresholds, and policy enforcement. Address report statistics, trend analysis, and system improvement recommendations based on report patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cron Jobs & Background Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for VSocial's cron job system and background processing capabilities. Document scheduled task execution, system maintenance automation, and periodic cleanup operations. Explain how cron jobs are configured and managed within the SvelteKit server environment. Include examples of automated tasks such as signal cleanup, user session management, and system health checks. Address cron job scheduling strategies, error handling, logging mechanisms, and monitoring approaches for reliable background processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's routing system and navigation patterns. Explain SvelteKit's file-based routing, dynamic routes, and nested layouts. Document route guards, authentication protection, and redirect mechanisms. Detail navigation patterns, programmatic navigation, and route parameters handling. Include examples of implementing protected routes, conditional rendering, and navigation state management. Address SEO considerations, meta tags, and progressive enhancement. Document error handling in routing, loading states, and fallback mechanisms for failed navigations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive production configuration documentation for VSocial deployment. Detail nginx reverse proxy setup, SSL/TLS certificate management, and HTTPS configuration. Document environment variable management, secret handling, and configuration security. Explain static asset optimization, build configuration for production, and asset bundling strategies. Include practical examples of load balancer configuration, caching strategies, and CDN integration. Address security headers, CORS configuration, and rate limiting setup. Document performance tuning, memory optimization, and resource allocation for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Messaging & Communication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for VSocial's messaging and notification systems. Document direct message creation, group chat management, message threading, and real-time communication endpoints. Include message attachment handling, read receipts, typing indicators, and message reactions. Document notification delivery mechanisms, subscription management, and push notification integration. Provide examples of WebSocket connections for real-time messaging, message encryption patterns, and privacy controls. Include rate limiting for spam prevention and message history management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Post Interactions & Reactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for post interaction and reaction systems. Document like/unlike functionality, custom reaction types, and reaction counting mechanisms. Explain the notification system triggered by reactions, including user-to-user notifications and real-time updates. Detail the comment system integration, including hierarchical comments, reply functionality, and comment reactions. Cover save/unsave functionality for user bookmarking posts. Include comprehensive API documentation for all interaction endpoints, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Provide examples of reaction workflows, comment threading, and notification handling. Address performance optimization for high-volume interactions and real-time synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stories & Reels Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Stories and Reels content models. Detail the stories table structure including user_id, media_url, media_type, caption, duration_seconds, view_count, background_color, and automatic expiration (expires_at). Document the story_highlights system for organizing story collections with highlights table and highlight_items for individual story items. Explain the reels table with video_url, thumbnail_url, caption, duration_seconds, view_count, engagement metrics, and activation status. Cover story expiration mechanisms, highlight organization patterns, and media processing workflows. Include indexing strategies for active/expiring stories, query patterns for story feeds, and integration with user following systems. Document privacy considerations for ephemeral content and storage optimization for media assets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design & Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for VSocial's database schema. Detail entity relationships, field definitions, and data types for all core models including users, posts, stories, messages, marketplace items, and gig listings. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Explain data validation rules and business rules implemented at the database level. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document data access patterns, caching strategies, and performance considerations. Specify data lifecycle, retention policies, and archival rules. Include data migration paths and version management. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data flow documentation for VSocial's communication patterns. Document the request-response cycle from frontend components through API routes to database operations. Explain real-time data flow for messaging and notifications using WebSocket connections. Detail state synchronization between frontend stores and backend services. Cover asynchronous processing patterns for background jobs and cron tasks. Document error propagation, retry mechanisms, and failure handling strategies. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user workflows like posting content, sending messages, and real-time interactions. Address data validation, sanitization, and transformation at each communication boundary.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for VSocial's authentication endpoints. Document all authentication-related HTTP endpoints including POST /api/auth/register, POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/auth/logout, and GET /api/auth/check. Detail request/response schemas, required parameters, authentication requirements, and success/error responses. Include curl examples, SDK usage patterns, and client-side integration guides. Explain session management through API calls, token refresh mechanisms, and logout procedures. Address rate limiting, security headers, and CORS configuration for authentication endpoints. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common authentication errors and client-side authentication state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Commerce & Marketplace Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for VSocial's commerce and marketplace models including marketplace categories, listings, gig board, and transaction systems. Document product catalog management, pricing structures, and inventory handling. Explain freelancer gig creation, application management, and offer systems. Detail wallet architecture, transaction processing, and payment workflows. Cover listing lifecycle from creation to expiration, moderation flags, and quality scoring. Document offer negotiation, contract management, and dispute resolution mechanisms. Include commission calculations, fee structures, and revenue sharing models. Provide examples of marketplace analytics, trending products, and recommendation algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hashtags & Content Discovery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for VSocial's hashtag system and content discovery mechanisms. Detail hashtag parsing, indexing, and trending algorithm implementation. Document content recommendation engines, algorithmic feed personalization, and content discovery features. Explain search functionality including text search, hashtag search, user search, and content categorization. Include API endpoints for search operations, trending hashtags, and personalized content feeds. Provide examples of hashtag usage patterns, content categorization, and discovery workflows. Address performance considerations for search indexing, real-time trend calculation, and content relevance ranking. Document content moderation filters and safe discovery features for family-friendly content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Persistence & Storage
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's message storage and persistence architecture. Detail the database schema for messages, conversations, and related metadata tables. Explain message lifecycle management including creation, modification, deletion, and archival processes. Document the cursor-based pagination implementation for efficient message retrieval and scrolling. Cover read receipt tracking, message indexing strategies, and database optimization techniques. Include data retention policies, cleanup procedures, and storage capacity management. Address backup and recovery procedures for message data, data integrity checks, and consistency guarantees across distributed systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Security & Compliance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for financial security measures and regulatory compliance in VSocial's wallet system. Detail fraud prevention mechanisms, including transaction monitoring, suspicious activity detection, and user verification requirements. Document security measures for financial data protection, encryption at rest and in transit, and secure API endpoints. Explain compliance requirements for financial services, including KYC/AML procedures, transaction reporting, and audit trail preservation. Include examples of security implementations, threat mitigation strategies, and incident response procedures. Address data retention policies, financial record keeping, and regulatory reporting capabilities. Document backup and disaster recovery procedures for financial data.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Settings & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for VSocial's administrative settings and system configuration interface. Detail the system-wide configuration options including platform policies, feature toggles, content guidelines, and operational parameters. Document the settings management interface with configuration categories, validation rules, and backup/restore functionality. Explain the system health monitoring tools, performance metrics, and administrative alerts. Include practical examples of configuration changes, system updates, and emergency procedures. Address security settings, compliance configurations, and integration point management for third-party services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Live Updates & Notifications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's live update and notification delivery system. Detail real-time notification mechanisms, push notification handling, and live feed synchronization. Document notification types, delivery strategies, and user preference management. Explain WebSocket-based live updates for social interactions, comment notifications, and system alerts. Include practical examples of notification routing, batch processing, and delivery confirmation mechanisms. Address notification throttling, user opt-out handling, and performance optimization for high-volume notification scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance & Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for frontend performance optimization and best practices in VSocial. Detail code splitting strategies, lazy loading implementations, and bundle optimization techniques. Document component performance patterns, reactive updates optimization, and memory management. Explain caching strategies, asset optimization, and build-time optimizations. Include practical examples of performance monitoring, profiling techniques, and bottleneck identification. Address critical rendering path optimization, hydration strategies, and server-side rendering considerations. Document browser compatibility, polyfill usage, and progressive enhancement approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive monitoring and logging documentation for VSocial production deployment. Detail application health check endpoints, system metrics collection, and performance monitoring strategies. Document log aggregation, structured logging implementation, and log rotation policies. Explain alerting configuration, error tracking, and incident response procedures. Include practical examples of monitoring dashboards, custom metrics, and integration with monitoring platforms. Address distributed tracing, performance profiling, and bottleneck identification. Document backup verification, disaster recovery testing, and system reliability measurements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Marketplace & Gigs API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for VSocial's e-commerce and freelance marketplace endpoints. Document product listing creation, search, and purchase workflows with payment processing integration. Include gig posting, application management, and commission handling endpoints. Document wallet operations, transaction processing, and monetization features. Provide examples of media upload for listings, pricing models, and escrow mechanisms. Include order management, review systems, and dispute resolution APIs. Document tax calculation, currency conversion, and international payment support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Poll Voting System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create specialized documentation for the poll and voting system integrated within posts. Detail poll creation workflow, question formatting, and option management. Explain vote casting mechanisms, user eligibility checks, and vote validation. Document poll metadata storage, vote counting algorithms, and result calculation. Cover poll lifecycle management, including poll expiration, modification restrictions, and result visibility. Include API documentation for poll endpoints, request schemas, and response formats. Provide examples of creating polls with different option types, voting workflows, and result retrieval. Address security considerations for vote integrity, preventing multiple voting, and poll manipulation prevention.

wiki_catalogs.name: Messaging & Conversation Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the messaging and conversation system including conversations, conversation_participants, messages_new, message_reactions, and message_read_receipts. Document the conversations table with type classification (direct message vs group), group metadata, creator tracking, and last message timestamps. Explain the many-to-many conversation_participants relationship with admin privileges and join timestamps. Detail the messages_new table with rich content support including text bodies, voice messages, media attachments, reply threading, and deletion handling. Document the message_reactions system for emoji responses and message_read_receipts for delivery/read tracking. Cover conversation lifecycle management, participant management, message ordering, and real-time communication patterns. Include indexing strategies for message retrieval, privacy considerations for conversation data, and integration with push notification systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's authentication and authorization system. Explain JWT-based session management, password hashing with bcryptjs, and token lifecycle. Document the complete authentication flow from registration to login to protected route access. Detail user roles, permissions, and access control mechanisms. Include API endpoint specifications for authentication routes, request/response schemas, and error handling. Explain session management, token refresh strategies, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of authentication middleware usage and protected resource access. Address common security vulnerabilities and mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Authentication Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for frontend authentication integration in VSocial. Explain the Svelte 5 store implementation for authentication state management, including reactive user data, session tracking, and automatic token handling. Document client-side authentication flows, login state persistence, and automatic redirect handling. Detail the integration between frontend stores and backend authentication endpoints, including error propagation and loading states. Include examples of protected route guards, conditional UI rendering based on authentication status, and authentication-aware component patterns. Address browser storage considerations, token refresh strategies, and offline authentication scenarios. Provide guidance on extending authentication functionality and integrating with third-party authentication providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: System & Administrative Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's system and administrative models including notifications, reports, moderation tools, and system settings. Document notification types, delivery mechanisms, and user preference management. Explain reporting system for content moderation, user complaints, and policy violations. Detail administrative controls, permission systems, and audit logging. Cover system configuration management, feature toggles, and environment-specific settings. Document push notification subscriptions, web push protocol implementation, and subscription management. Include spam detection mechanisms, content filtering, and automated moderation tools. Provide examples of system monitoring, maintenance procedures, and troubleshooting workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media & Content API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for VSocial's media handling and content management endpoints. Document file upload mechanisms, media processing pipelines, and content moderation workflows. Include story creation, reel upload, and media transcoding endpoints. Provide examples of multipart form data handling, chunked uploads, and progress tracking. Document image optimization, video compression, and CDN integration patterns. Include content expiration policies, storage management, and cleanup procedures. Address copyright protection, watermarking, and content ownership verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Marketplace & Gigs Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Marketplace and Freelance Gigs entities including marketplace_categories, marketplace_listings, listing_media, gigs, and gig_applications. Document the marketplace_categories hierarchy with parent-child relationships, slugs, and categorization. Detail the marketplace_listings table with user_id, category_id, title, description, pricing (price, currency), condition, location, status management, view tracking, and expiration handling. Explain the listing_media system for product image galleries. Document the gigs system for freelance services including title, description, category, pricing range, application management, and expiration. Cover the gig_applications workflow for bid management and candidate screening. Include business rules for listing moderation, pricing validation, application status tracking, and integration with payment systems. Address search and filtering capabilities, category management, and marketplace analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's social core features. Detail the complete social networking functionality including posts creation and management, timeline/feed algorithms, commenting and reaction systems, and following/follower relationships. Document API endpoints for each feature with request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Explain content discovery mechanisms, hashtag systems, and social graph management. Include practical examples of social interactions, data models, and real-time updates. Address performance considerations for large-scale social networks and content moderation features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Migration & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for VSocial's database migration and management procedures. Document the migration system architecture, version control, and rollback mechanisms. Explain schema evolution patterns, data transformation procedures, and backward compatibility maintenance. Detail migration execution, dependency management, and error handling strategies. Cover database backup procedures, restoration processes, and disaster recovery planning. Document performance monitoring, index optimization, and maintenance scheduling. Include deployment procedures, zero-downtime migrations, and production safety measures. Provide troubleshooting guides for common migration issues and database corruption scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administration & Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for VSocial's administrative and system management endpoints. Document user moderation tools, content management interfaces, and system administration workflows. Include report handling, appeal processes, and automated moderation systems. Document system settings management, feature toggles, and configuration endpoints. Provide examples of bulk operations, audit logging, and compliance reporting. Include permission-based access controls, role management, and administrative workflow automation. Address data export, backup procedures, and disaster recovery APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wallet & Transactions Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Wallet and Transaction system including wallets, wallet_transactions, and transactions tables. Document the wallets table with user_id relationships, balance tracking, and timestamp management. Detail the wallet_transactions table for transaction history with type categorization, amount tracking, descriptions, and status management. Explain the transactions table for detailed financial records with reference_id linking and audit trail capabilities. Cover transaction types, balance calculations, funding mechanisms, and withdrawal processes. Document security considerations for financial data, transaction validation rules, and reconciliation procedures. Include query patterns for balance reporting, transaction history, and financial analytics. Address integration with payment processors, fraud detection triggers, and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stories & Reels System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's stories and reels functionality. Explain story creation, expiration policies, highlight integration, and ephemeral content management. Document reels system for video content including upload handling, processing workflows, and playback features. Detail API endpoints for media management, story scheduling, and content expiration. Include practical examples of media processing, storage optimization, and delivery mechanisms. Address technical challenges specific to ephemeral content, media transcoding, and CDN integration for optimal user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility & Search API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for VSocial's utility and system service endpoints. Document search indexing, content discovery, and recommendation algorithms. Include cron job management, system health monitoring, and maintenance operations. Document analytics collection, performance metrics, and system diagnostics endpoints. Provide examples of search query optimization, autocomplete functionality, and trending content detection. Include system status endpoints, uptime monitoring, and alerting mechanisms. Address caching strategies, performance optimization, and resource utilization tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notifications & System Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Notifications and System entities including notifications, system_settings, email_tokens, oauth_accounts, blocked_users, snoozed_users, web_push_subscriptions, and sponsored_posts. Document the notifications table with recipient tracking, actor references, type categorization, entity linking, and read status management. Detail the system_settings table for global configuration management with key-value pairs and feature toggles. Explain the email_tokens system for authentication and verification workflows. Document the oauth_accounts table for third-party authentication integration. Cover user blocking and snoozing systems for privacy management. Detail web_push_subscriptions for browser notification support and sponsored_posts for advertising system. Include indexing strategies for notification delivery, privacy controls, spam prevention, and system configuration management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Messaging & Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's messaging and communication features. Detail direct messaging system including message threading, read receipts, and media sharing. Document group chat functionality with participant management, permissions, and moderation tools. Explain real-time communication architecture using WebSocket integration. Include API specifications for message CRUD operations, group management, and presence detection. Address scalability challenges for real-time messaging, message persistence, and offline message delivery. Provide examples of message encryption, spam prevention, and content moderation in chat contexts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Marketplace & Freelance Gigs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's marketplace and freelance gig platforms. Explain product listing management, categorization, search functionality, and transaction processing. Document freelance gig system including posting, application management, offer handling, and review systems. Detail API endpoints for e-commerce operations, payment processing integration, and inventory management. Include practical examples of marketplace moderation, seller/buyer protection, and dispute resolution. Address technical challenges around media handling for products, order fulfillment tracking, and financial transaction security.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wallet & Transactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's wallet and monetization system. Detail balance management, transaction history, tip functionality, and transfer mechanisms. Document API endpoints for wallet operations, payment processing, and financial reporting. Explain integration with external payment processors and cryptocurrency support if applicable. Include practical examples of transaction workflows, fraud prevention measures, and audit trail maintenance. Address regulatory compliance considerations, tax reporting features, and financial data security. Document wallet security measures, backup and recovery procedures, and dispute resolution processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administration & Moderation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's administrative and moderation tools. Explain user management interface, content moderation workflows, report handling systems, and system settings administration. Document admin panel functionality including user profiling, content flagging, automated moderation tools, and manual review processes. Detail API endpoints for administrative operations, bulk user management, and system analytics. Include practical examples of moderation workflows, appeal processes, and community guideline enforcement. Address scalability considerations for large user bases and automated content moderation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's real-time features including WebSocket integration, signaling servers, and live updates. Detail the RTC (Real-time Communication) system for voice/video calls, real-time messaging synchronization, and live notifications. Document WebSocket connection handling, message broadcasting, and presence management. Explain cron job system for scheduled tasks, background processing, and system maintenance. Include practical examples of real-time event handling, connection pooling, and fault tolerance. Address scalability challenges for real-time features, load balancing strategies, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture & Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's frontend architecture and component system. Explain Svelte 5 reactivity with runes, component composition patterns, and state management using stores. Document the component library structure, reusable UI components, and design system implementation. Detail routing system, layout management, and page transitions. Include practical examples of component development, prop handling, event management, and integration patterns. Address performance optimization techniques, code splitting strategies, and accessibility compliance. Document testing approaches for frontend components and integration with backend APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all VSocial REST endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint group. Include detailed parameter specifications, validation rules, and error response formats. Organize endpoints by functional domains (authentication, social, messaging, marketplace, etc.). Provide practical examples of API usage, client implementation guidelines, and SDK integration patterns. Document rate limiting, pagination strategies, and versioning approaches. Include security considerations, CORS configuration, and API testing methodologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for VSocial. Detail Docker containerization strategy, Docker Compose orchestration, and production deployment configurations. Document environment variable management, SSL/TLS configuration, and reverse proxy setup with nginx. Explain CI/CD pipeline considerations, automated testing integration, and deployment automation. Include practical examples of production hardening, monitoring setup, and logging configuration. Address scalability considerations, load balancing strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Document backup procedures, database maintenance, and system monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing & Code Quality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VSocial's testing strategy and code quality practices. Detail unit testing approach using Vitest, test organization patterns, and mocking strategies. Document code quality tools including ESLint configuration, Prettier formatting, and pre-commit hooks. Explain testing best practices for Svelte components, API endpoints, and database operations. Include practical examples of test writing, continuous integration setup, and code coverage measurement. Address performance testing, security testing considerations, and accessibility testing approaches. Document debugging techniques and troubleshooting methodologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Tools & Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VSocial's development tools and utility scripts. Explain database migration system including up/down migrations, seed data management, and schema evolution. Document utility scripts for database maintenance, legacy cleanup, and system updates. Detail development workflow tools, build configuration, and local development environment setup. Include practical examples of using migration scripts, seed data creation, and database troubleshooting. Address backup and restore procedures, data export/import capabilities, and system maintenance workflows. Document debugging tools, performance profiling techniques, and development environment optimization.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# VSocial: Comprehensive Social Network Platform

## Project Introduction

VSocial is a full-stack social networking application built with modern web technologies, designed specifically for the virtual creator community. The platform serves as a comprehensive social hub supporting VTubers, streamers, digital artists, and their communities with an extensive feature set including posts, stories, reels, real-time messaging, marketplace functionality, and freelance gig boards.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Community-Centric Design**: Create a specialized social platform for virtual creators and their audiences
- **Performance Optimization**: Deliver high-performance social interactions through optimized database design and efficient APIs
- **Feature-Rich Experience**: Provide comprehensive social networking capabilities in a single integrated platform
- **Developer-Friendly Architecture**: Maintain clean, maintainable code with clear separation of concerns

### Target Audience

- Virtual YouTubers and streamers
- Digital artists and content creators
- Gaming communities and influencers
- Social media enthusiasts seeking specialized virtual content platforms

## Technical Architecture

VSocial employs a modern full-stack architecture built on SvelteKit, utilizing a hybrid SSR/CSR rendering approach for optimal performance and user experience.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[SvelteKit Frontend]
    B --> C[API Routes]
    C --> D[Database Layer]
    D --> E[(SQLite/LibSQL)]
    
    F[Server Hooks] --> G[Authentication]
    F --> H[Cron Jobs]
    F --> I[Security Headers]
    
    C --> F
    G --> D
    H --> D
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture follows a clear separation of concerns with distinct layers:

**Frontend Layer**: Svelte 5 with SvelteKit providing reactive UI components and state management
**Backend Layer**: Node.js-based API routes handling business logic and data operations  
**Data Layer**: SQLite database with custom ORM wrapper supporting both local and remote connections
**Infrastructure Layer**: Docker containerization with automated deployment workflows

### Design Patterns

- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations encapsulated in dedicated modules
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic database driver selection between @libsql/client and better-sqlite3
- **Observer Pattern**: Reactive state management using Svelte 5 runes
- **Middleware Pattern**: Centralized authentication and security through server hooks

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application follows SvelteKit's conventional routing structure with clear entry points:

**Frontend Application**: `frontend/src/app.html` - Main HTML template with SEO optimization and theme configuration
**Server Initialization**: `frontend/src/hooks.server.js` - Central server configuration with security headers and cron job management
**Database Abstraction**: `frontend/src/lib/server/db.js` - Unified database interface supporting multiple drivers

### Core Modules

**Authentication System**: `frontend/src/routes/api/auth[action]/+server.js` - Complete user authentication with JWT token management and session handling
**Database Schema**: `schema_sqlite.sql` - Comprehensive relational schema supporting all social features including users, posts, messaging, marketplace, and notifications
**API Endpoints**: Modular API structure under `frontend/src/routes/api/` supporting all major platform features

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Management**: `.env` file with configurable database URLs, authentication tokens, and feature flags
**Build Configuration**: `frontend/svelte.config.js` - SvelteKit configuration with Tailwind CSS integration and component compilation settings
**Development Tools**: ESLint and Prettier configurations for consistent code quality across the development team

### External Dependencies

The project maintains a focused dependency set optimized for performance and security:

**Core Dependencies**:
- `@libsql/client`: Primary database driver with remote capability
- `bcryptjs`: Password hashing for secure authentication
- `jsonwebtoken`: JWT token generation and validation
- `helmet`: Security headers for enhanced protection

**Development Dependencies**:
- `vitest`: Unit testing framework with SvelteKit integration
- `@sveltejs/kit`: SvelteKit framework with SSR/SSG capabilities
- `tailwindcss`: Utility-first CSS framework for rapid UI development

### Integration Points

**Database Integration**: Seamless switching between local SQLite and remote LibSQL databases through unified API abstraction
**Authentication Integration**: JWT-based authentication with automatic session management and token refresh
**File Upload Integration**: Secure file handling with configurable upload directories and size limits
**Real-time Features**: WebSocket integration for live messaging and notifications

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[User Interface] --> B[API Layer]
    B --> C[Business Logic]
    C --> D[Database Operations]
    D --> E[File Storage]
    
    F[Authentication] --> B
    G[Security Middleware] --> B
    H[Cache Layer] --> C
    
    B --> F
    B --> G
    C --> H
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

VSocial provides a comprehensive social networking experience through its modular feature architecture:

**Social Core Features**:
- Real-time posts with likes, comments, and sharing capabilities
- Interactive stories with expiration and engagement tracking
- Short-form video content (Reels) with discovery algorithms
- Follow/unfollow relationships with social graph management

**Communication Features**:
- Real-time direct messaging with media support
- Group chat functionality with participant management
- Voice note recording and playback capabilities
- Message reactions and read receipts

**E-commerce Features**:
- Marketplace for buying and selling digital and physical goods
- Freelance gig board for commission-based work
- Wallet system with transaction history and balance management
- Category-based product organization with filtering

**Administrative Features**:
- User moderation tools and reporting systems
- Content management for administrators
- System settings and configuration management
- Analytics and usage tracking

### Implementation Highlights

**Performance Optimization**: `frontend/src/lib/server/db.js` - Custom database abstraction layer supporting both local and remote connections with automatic failover
**Security Implementation**: Comprehensive authentication middleware with JWT token validation and session management
**Real-time Communication**: WebSocket integration for live messaging and notification delivery
**Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with adaptive layouts and touch-friendly interfaces

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Authentication
    Authentication --> Feed: Login Success
    Authentication --> Setup: First Time User
    Authentication --> Install: Fresh Installation
    
    Feed --> Posts: View Timeline
    Feed --> Messages: Open Chat
    Feed --> Marketplace: Browse Items
    Feed --> Reels: Watch Videos
    Feed --> Stories: View Stories
    
    Posts --> CreatePost: New Post
    Posts --> ViewComments: Comment
    Posts --> LikePost: Like Action
    
    Messages --> SendMessage: Send Message
    Messages --> MediaUpload: Attach Files
    Messages --> GroupChat: Create Group
    
    Marketplace --> BrowseListings: Search Items
    Marketplace --> PurchaseItem: Buy Product
    Marketplace --> SellItem: List Item
    
    Setup --> ProfileCompletion: Complete Profile
    Setup --> FeatureEnable: Enable Features
    Setup --> AdminConfig: Configure Settings
    
    Feed --> [*]: Logout
    Setup --> [*]: Complete
    Install --> [*]: Ready
```

The VSocial platform represents a sophisticated social networking solution that balances feature richness with technical excellence. Its modular architecture, performance optimizations, and comprehensive feature set position it as a strong foundation for building the next generation of social platforms, particularly tailored for the growing virtual creator economy.

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [app.html](frontend/src/app.html)
- [hooks.server.js](frontend/src/hooks.server.js)
- [db.js](frontend/src/lib/server/db.js)
- [schema_sqlite.sql](schema_sqlite.sql)
- [auth+server.js](frontend/src/routes/api/auth%5Baction%5D/+server.js)

</blog>
